content.content:
# cockroachdb/major-upgrade-finalize
# Upgrade CockroachDB cluster from v{{params.from_version}} to v{{params.to_version}} and finalize the major upgrade

prompt: |
  A CockroachDB cluster is running v{{params.from_version}} binaries and is ready for a major upgrade.

  Your task:
  Upgrade the cluster to v{{params.to_version}} and finalize the major upgrade.

  Requirements:
  - Upgrade binaries first, then finalize the major upgrade.
  - Keep the cluster available throughout.

  Useful commands:
  ```bash
  kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods
  kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SHOW CLUSTER SETTING version;"
  ```

  Verification:
  1. All pods on v{{params.to_version}}:
     kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}: {.spec.containers[0].image}{"\n"}{end}'

  2. Cluster version finalized:
     kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- \
       ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SHOW CLUSTER SETTING version;"
     (Should show '24.1' not '{{params.from_version}}')

  3. Preserve downgrade option cleared:
     kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- \
       ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SHOW CLUSTER SETTING cluster.preserve_downgrade_option;"
     (Should show empty)

  4. Cluster healthy after finalization:
     kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- \
       ./cockroach node status --insecure

  5. Can use v24.1 features:
     kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- \
       ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SELECT version();"

params:
  from_version:
    default: "23.2"
    description: CockroachDB version the cluster starts on before the upgrade
  to_version:
    default: 24.1.0
    description: Target CockroachDB version to upgrade and finalize to

namespace_contract:
  required_roles: []

metrics:
  - time_to_first_mutation
  - destructive_ops

preconditionUnits:
  - name: crdb_env_ready
    on_probe_fail: skip
    probe:
      # Non-destructive skip-if-running probe. The env PERSISTS across workflow
      # stages: when a cockroachdb cluster is already present (inherited from a
      # prior stage), skip the destructive apply so accumulated state is
      # preserved. The apply (which deletes+recreates the namespace) runs ONLY on
      # an empty namespace. NOTE: this case needs a pre-upgrade
      # v{{params.from_version}} base; if it is chained after a stage that already
      # upgraded/finalized the cluster it has nothing to upgrade — that is a
      # workflow-ordering concern to be curated separately, NOT something to fix
      # by redeploying and wiping the inherited state.
      # AUTHORITATIVE skip-if-running probe (Pattern 7): succeed when the
      # canonical StatefulSet exists OR any cockroach pod is Running, so the
      # destructive namespace rebuild below is skipped on an inherited cluster
      # even if its pods are momentarily not Running (mid-restart from a prior
      # stage) -- the StatefulSet persists across that window. The bare
      # `grep -c Running` could miss such a cluster and clobber inherited state.
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'kubectl -n cockroachdb get statefulset crdb-cluster >/dev/null 2>&1 || kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods 2>/dev/null | grep -q Running'"
        timeout_sec: 10
    apply:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'kubectl delete namespace cockroachdb --ignore-not-found=true --wait=false; kubectl wait --for=delete namespace/cockroachdb --timeout=400s || true'"
        timeout_sec: 460
        sleep: 2
      - command: "kubectl create namespace cockroachdb"
        sleep: 1
      - command: "kubectl -n cockroachdb apply -f cases/cockroachdb/major-upgrade-finalize/resource/"
        sleep: 5
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'for i in $(seq 1 30); do count=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pod -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cockroachdb --no-headers 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d \" \"); if [ \"${count}\" -ge 1 ]; then exit 0; fi; sleep 2; done; exit 1'"
        sleep: 1
      - command: "kubectl -n cockroachdb wait --for=jsonpath={.status.phase}=Running pod -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cockroachdb --timeout=300s"
        sleep: 5
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'for i in $(seq 1 30); do kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach init --insecure --host=crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local && exit 0; sleep 3; done; exit 1'"
        sleep: 2
      - command: "kubectl -n cockroachdb wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cockroachdb --timeout=600s"
        timeout_sec: 640
        sleep: 10
      - command: "kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e \"SET CLUSTER SETTING cluster.preserve_downgrade_option = '{{params.from_version}}';\""
        sleep: 2
    verify:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'flag=--insecure; kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ls /cockroach/cockroach-certs/ca.crt >/dev/null 2>&1 && flag=--certs-dir=/cockroach/cockroach-certs; kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql $flag -e \"SELECT 1;\" 2>/dev/null'"
        timeout_sec: 15

oracle:
  verify:
    commands:
      - command: "python3 cases/cockroachdb/major-upgrade-finalize/oracle/oracle.py"
        timeout_sec: 120

#!/usr/bin/env python3
# Verify the cluster was upgraded AND finalized to the configured target. The
# target version comes from the case param (BENCH_PARAM_TO_VERSION): the pod
# image must be that full version and the finalized logical cluster version must
# be its major.minor. Standalone (default param) this behaves identically to the
# old hardcoded check.
import json
import os
import subprocess
import sys


TO_VERSION = os.environ.get("BENCH_PARAM_TO_VERSION", "24.1.0")
TARGET_IMAGE = f"cockroachdb/cockroach:v{TO_VERSION}"
# Logical cluster version is major.minor (e.g. "24.1" for binary "24.1.0").
TARGET_VERSION = ".".join(TO_VERSION.split(".")[:2])


def run(cmd):
    return subprocess.run(cmd, text=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE)


_CONN_FLAG = None


def conn_flag():
    """Return the right cockroach SQL connection flag for the live cluster.

    Standalone this case runs against an INSECURE cluster (`--insecure`). But in
    a workflow this stage can inherit a SECURE cluster left running by a prior
    stage (e.g. certificate-rotation), whose precondition probe sees pods already
    Running and skips its own insecure redeploy. A hardcoded `--insecure` then
    fails with an SSL authentication error. Detect the mode once by checking for
    the mounted certs dir and connect accordingly so the same oracle works in
    both contexts. Mirrors cockroachdb/cluster-settings/oracle/oracle.py.
    """
    global _CONN_FLAG
    if _CONN_FLAG is not None:
        return _CONN_FLAG
    probe = run([
        "kubectl", "-n", "cockroachdb", "--request-timeout=15s", "exec",
        "crdb-cluster-0", "--", "ls", "/cockroach/cockroach-certs/ca.crt",
    ])
    if probe.returncode == 0:
        _CONN_FLAG = "--certs-dir=/cockroach/cockroach-certs"
    else:
        _CONN_FLAG = "--insecure"
    return _CONN_FLAG


_EXPECTED_NODES = None


def expected_nodes():
    """Resolve how many live nodes the cluster should have.

    This stage UPGRADES an inherited cluster; it does not change topology, so the
    expected count adapts to whatever was inherited: an explicit param override
    (BENCH_PARAM_EXPECTED_NODES / _REPLICA_COUNT) wins, else the live
    StatefulSet's DESIRED size (spec.replicas), else the old hardcoded 3. Using
    the desired size (not readyReplicas) keeps the check honest — if a node is
    lost during the upgrade, live_nodes < spec.replicas still fails. Only the
    count target adapts; every node must still be live and on the target image.
    Cached.
    """
    global _EXPECTED_NODES
    if _EXPECTED_NODES is not None:
        return _EXPECTED_NODES
    override = (
        os.environ.get("BENCH_PARAM_EXPECTED_NODES")
        or os.environ.get("BENCH_PARAM_REPLICA_COUNT")
    )
    if override and override.strip().isdigit():
        _EXPECTED_NODES = int(override)
        return _EXPECTED_NODES
    result = run([
        "kubectl", "-n", "cockroachdb", "get", "statefulset", "crdb-cluster",
        "-o", "jsonpath={.spec.replicas}",
    ])
    if result.returncode == 0 and result.stdout.strip().isdigit():
        live = int(result.stdout.strip())
        if live > 0:
            _EXPECTED_NODES = live
            return _EXPECTED_NODES
    _EXPECTED_NODES = 3
    return _EXPECTED_NODES


def parse_tsv(output):
    lines = [line.strip() for line in output.splitlines() if line.strip()]
    if not lines:
        return [], []
    header = lines[0].split("\t")
    rows = [line.split("\t") for line in lines[1:]]
    return header, rows


def to_bool(value):
    return str(value).strip().lower() in ("true", "t", "1", "yes")


def evaluate():
    """One full snapshot of the major-upgrade-finalize checks; returns error list."""
    errors = []

    cmd = [
        "kubectl",
        "-n",
        "cockroachdb",
        "get",
        "pods",
        "-l",
        "app.kubernetes.io/name=cockroachdb",
        "-o",
        "json",
    ]
    result = run(cmd)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        errors.append(result.stderr.strip() or "Failed to read pods")
    else:
        try:
            data = json.loads(result.stdout)
            pods = data.get("items", [])
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            errors.append("Failed to parse pod list")
            pods = []
        if len(pods) != expected_nodes():
            errors.append(f"Expected {expected_nodes()} pods, found {len(pods)}")
        for pod in pods:
            name = pod.get("metadata", {}).get("name", "unknown")
            containers = pod.get("spec", {}).get("containers", [])
            if not containers:
                errors.append(f"No containers found in pod {name}")
                continue
            image = containers[0].get("image") or ""
            # Accept any patch of the target MAJOR version (e.g. v24.1.1 for a 24.1
            # finalize). A prior workflow upgrade stage or the agent may validly
            # land on a different 24.1.x patch, and a major upgrade can't be
            # downgraded to an exact patch. The logical-cluster-version finalize
            # check below still enforces the major upgrade; only the patch is free.
            img_majmin = ".".join(image.split(":")[-1].lstrip("v").split(".")[:2])
            if img_majmin != TARGET_VERSION:
                errors.append(f"Pod {name} image is {image} (expected v{TARGET_VERSION}.x)")

    cmd = [
        "kubectl",
        "-n",
        "cockroachdb",
        "exec",
        "crdb-cluster-0",
        "--",
        "./cockroach",
        "sql",
        conn_flag(),
        "-e",
        "SHOW CLUSTER SETTING version;",
    ]
    result = run(cmd)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        errors.append(result.stderr.strip() or "Failed to check cluster version")
    elif TARGET_VERSION not in result.stdout:
        errors.append("Cluster version not finalized")

    cmd = [
        "kubectl",
        "-n",
        "cockroachdb",
        "exec",
        "crdb-cluster-0",
        "--",
        "./cockroach",
        "sql",
        conn_flag(),
        "--format=tsv",
        "-e",
        "SHOW CLUSTER SETTING cluster.preserve_downgrade_option;",
    ]
    result = run(cmd)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        errors.append(result.stderr.strip() or "Failed to check preserve_downgrade_option")
    else:
        lines = []
        for line in result.stdout.splitlines():
            stripped = line.strip()
            if not stripped:
                continue
            if stripped == "cluster.preserve_downgrade_option":
                continue
            if set(stripped) == {"-"}:
                continue
            lines.append(stripped)
        value = lines[-1] if lines else ""
        if value not in ("", "NULL", "[]"):
            errors.append("preserve_downgrade_option not cleared")

    cmd = [
        "kubectl",
        "-n",
        "cockroachdb",
        "exec",
        "crdb-cluster-0",
        "--",
        "./cockroach",
        "node",
        "status",
        conn_flag(),
        "--format=tsv",
    ]
    result = run(cmd)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        errors.append(result.stderr.strip() or "Failed to read node status")
    else:
        header, rows = parse_tsv(result.stdout)
        if not header:
            errors.append("Empty node status output")
        else:
            cols = {name: idx for idx, name in enumerate(header)}
            live_idx = cols.get("is_live")
            if live_idx is None:
                errors.append("Missing is_live column in node status output")
            else:
                live_nodes = 0
                for row in rows:
                    if len(row) <= live_idx:
                        continue
                    if to_bool(row[live_idx]):
                        live_nodes += 1
                if live_nodes != expected_nodes():
                    errors.append(f"Expected {expected_nodes()} live nodes, found {live_nodes}")

    return errors


def main():
    # A major-upgrade finalize restarts every node (one at a time); a
    # just-restarted node takes a short while to rejoin and report is_live. A
    # single snapshot can race that convergence and see e.g. "2 live nodes" on a
    # cluster healthily finishing its rollout (the same case passes at the prior
    # stages). Re-evaluate for up to ~70s and pass on the first clean snapshot.
    # This does not loosen the check -- a node that genuinely fails to rejoin
    # never becomes live, so the oracle still fails after the deadline.
    import time
    deadline = time.monotonic() + 70
    errors = evaluate()
    while errors and time.monotonic() < deadline:
        time.sleep(7)
        errors = evaluate()

    if errors:
        print("Major upgrade finalization verification failed:", file=sys.stderr)
        for error in errors:
            print(f"  - {error}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 1

    print("Major version upgrade finalized")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())
